# macs2_chipseq_callpeak

## What This Does
Call ChIP-seq peaks with MACS2/3 callpeak mode.

## Use This Skill When
- Use for ChIP-seq peak calling to identify protein-DNA binding sites.
- Capability: chipseq_analysis
- Analysis category: chipseq_analysis

## Do Not Use This Skill When
- Not for ATAC-seq (use macs2_atacseq_callpeak) or RNA-seq analysis.

## Required Inputs
- `control_bam` (path, required): Matched control/input BAM file.
- `genome_size` (string, required): Effective genome size (hs, mm, etc.).
- `name` (string, required): Output sample label.
- `output_dir` (path, required): Output directory.
- `treatment_bam` (path, required): Treatment BAM file.

## Input Types
- bam

## Expected Outputs
- bed
- tsv

## Tools And Requirements
- macs2
- `min_cores`: 2
- `min_ram_gb`: 8

## Execution Contract
The harness executes the typed contract in `contract.yaml`. Do not invent file paths, skip required inputs, or bypass the wrapper.

Wrapper command template:

```text
macs2 callpeak -t {treatment_bam} -c {control_bam} -f BAM -g {genome_size} -n {name} --outdir {output_dir}
```

## Validation Notes
The harness should verify required outputs exist and match the declared artifact roles before downstream steps use them.

## Source Notes

The source contract includes these notes:

Use for TF/histone ChIP-seq peak calling with treatment/control BAMs.

## Onboarding Metadata
- Source: https://macs3-project.github.io/MACS/docs/callpeak.html
